title: Writing: Pointing at Objects
learningGoal: Construct sentences identifying objects using 'Yeh' (this) and 'Woh' (that), and forming basic Yes/No questions.
instructions: In this exercise, practice the SOV structure with demonstrative pronouns (Yeh/Woh). Remember that to turn a statement into a Yes/No question, you just put the word 'Kya' (what) at the very beginning of the sentence.
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences into Hindi using the word bank:
1. This is a book.
2. That is a car.
3. Is this a computer?
4. Yes, this is a computer.
promptGoal: Building sentences with 'Yeh/Woh' and 'Kya' for Yes/No questions.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: यह किताब है। वह गाड़ी है। क्या यह कंप्यूटर है? हाँ, यह कंप्यूटर है।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Yeh kitaab hai. Woh gaadi hai. Kya yeh kampyutar hai? Haan, yeh kampyutar hai.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: This is a book. That is a car. Is this a computer? Yes, this is a computer.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect translation capturing the 'Kya' at the beginning for a Yes/No question.

evaluationCriteria.description: The verb 'hai' is placed at the end of every sentence.
Places 'Kya' at the start of the sentence to form a Yes/No question.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: यह किताब है। वह गाड़ी है। क्या यह कंप्यूटर है? हाँ, यह कंप्यूटर है।
acceptableAlternates: Yeh kitaab hai. Woh gaadi hai. Kya yeh kampyutar hai? Haan, yeh kampyutar hai.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict checking of 'Kya' placement.

criteria.name: Yes/No Question Logic
criteria.description: Must understand that 'Kya yeh kampyutar hai?' is the only way to say 'Is this a computer?'.

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You know how to identify objects and ask simple questions.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the Yes/No question structure. You do not move the verb like in English (Is this...?). You just put 'Kya' at the front.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Check your use of 'Yeh' vs 'Woh'. Yeh is for nearby objects (this), Woh is for far objects (that).

hintText: In Hindi, you don't use 'a' or 'an'. 'This is a book' translates literally to 'This book is'.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Hai yeh kampyutar?' (Trying to use English word order 'Is this...').
